journal_entry.description: Discounting of 90-day non-interest-bearing note receivable at bank (15% annual discount rate, 40 days remaining)
journal_entry.rationale: Ford discounted a $100,000 note receivable with 40 days remaining at 15% annual rate. Discount charge = $100,000 × 15% × (40/365) = $1,643.84. Net proceeds = $100,000 - $1,643.84 = $98,356.16. Since management determined this qualifies as derecognition under IFRS 9 (transfer of risks and rewards), we remove the note receivable asset and recognize the discount charge as a financing expense (Loss on Discounting of Note). Debit Cash for proceeds received, debit Loss on Discounting of Note for the discount charge, and credit Notes Receivable to derecognize the asset. This matches the tuple structure: 1 asset increase (cash), 1 expense increase (loss), and 1 asset decrease (note receivable).

output.reason: Ford discounts (sells) the note receivable to the bank and derecognizes it. The credit side records: (1) Notes Receivable leaving the books = asset decrease. The debit side would record cash received and potentially a loss on discounting, but we only classify credits here.

credit_classifier.reason: Ford discounts (sells) the note receivable to the bank and derecognizes it. The credit side records: (1) Notes Receivable leaving the books = asset decrease. The debit side would record cash received and potentially a loss on discounting, but we only classify credits here.

output.reason: Ford discounts the note at the bank and receives cash (asset increase = 1 line). The difference between the cash received and the carrying value of the note represents a loss on discounting/factoring (expense increase = 1 line). The note receivable will be credited (asset decrease on credit side). Under IFRS 9, since this qualifies as derecognition, the note is removed from Ford's books and any difference between proceeds and carrying value is recognized as a loss. Debit: Cash (asset increase) and Loss on Note Discounting (expense increase).

debit_classifier.reason: Ford discounts the note at the bank and receives cash (asset increase = 1 line). The difference between the cash received and the carrying value of the note represents a loss on discounting/factoring (expense increase = 1 line). The note receivable will be credited (asset decrease on credit side). Under IFRS 9, since this qualifies as derecognition, the note is removed from Ford's books and any difference between proceeds and carrying value is recognized as a loss. Debit: Cash (asset increase) and Loss on Note Discounting (expense increase).
